hard.ais_why:
• Per the 27 Oct rep note: 'Meeting the Ayala AI Chief AI Office, to see how we can propose an AI driven transformation strategy Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio (currently at Globe, and also is on the board at GCash- 12/13/14 in Manila 2nd degree connection2nd 7th Oct Good introduction meeting planned for Chief AI Officer (from Globe, but runs Ayala Conglom AI strategy) to demonstrate Zycus AI week of 20th- Carl/Akshay.' Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio, Chief AI Officer at Globe (who also runs Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy), is explicitly named as the AI-driven transformation strategy contact.
• Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' Per 21 Jul 2025: 'Add info on AI requested.' These are formal buyer-side AI-clarification requests within the tender process. Per the 13 Feb entry: 'Globe exec's part of GCash signing ceremony. RFP to be issued in 1 week.' GCash (Globe subsidiary, already a Zycus win via LOI) created a halo. Per 16 Mar 2026: 'Sent intro mail and meeting request.'
• Confidence is Medium. Globe has a named Chief AI Officer who is also Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy lead ‚Äî a senior AI sponsor. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRequest) but the captured dialogue includes buyer-originated AI clarifications and a Chief-AI-Officer audience. Caveats: BD_Engagement is Unengaged, multiple management changes have stalled the deal, and current state is reboot mode (May 2026). Score 7 reflects board-level AI sponsorship despite organisational disruption.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live but slow: procurement contacts Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) and Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) are still engaged and asking module questions, but the formal RFP has slipped for two years, the economic buyer (CFO Rizza Eala) has not been engaged since Jul 2024, and Carl Kimball halved the value from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026. Close by 31 Dec 2026 is not credible on current pace.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr 2024, ~798 days old. Stage Shortlisted; no submission-date fields populated, so time-in-stage is not computable from the series. Close 31 Dec 2026 is ~178 days out from 6 Jul 2026. RFP has been 'imminent' since Sep 2024 and was never issued; close date has slipped five times (2025-08-15 to 2025-10-15 to 2025-12-15 to 2026-01-15 to 2026-07-15 to 2026-12-31). To close by year-end still requires RFP issuance, multi-vendor evaluation, commercials, EB/CFO sign-off, InfoSec, ERP integration scoping and legal execution in under 26 weeks against an entrenched incumbent, which is at least 8 weeks behind the pace needed. Realistic close is H1 2027.
evidence: Last activity 15 Jun 2026 and inbound buyer emails through 2 Jun 2026 (Joseph Ortega asking whether the Supply Chain Risk Management module can run stand-alone) keep the deal inside the live window, but the most recent rep touches are largely Horizon event invites logged as 'Call Attempted'.

No formal RFP ever issued: Next Step history flags RFP 'imminent/due' across end-Sep 2024, end-Oct 2024 and Jan 2025, then reboot mode by May 2026; the most recent RFP mention is 17 Mar (Rog expects revised RFP soon, Zycus requirements are the basis).

Economic Buyer not engaged since 31 Jul 2024, when Carl met the then CEO Ernest, CFO Rizza Eala, Finance Controller Walter Ricarte and Jerry Cabiles; the 6 Jun 2026 plan targets procurement (Joseph Ortega, Jerry, Anton), not the CFO.
Deal value halved from 650,000 to 350,000 on 6 Jun 2026 (Carl Kimball) alongside the 16 Mar 2026 close-date push to 31 Dec 2026, a defensive commercial signal pointing to a phased or cost-cut scope.
SAP Ariba is the incumbent with renewals approaching (contract extended 3 months per 22 Aug 2024); the primary risk is a low-friction do-nothing renewal rather than a switch to a rival platform.

Repeated stalls: leadership turnover (new CEO/CFO/CIO settled 22 Aug 2024), group-wide capex prioritisation (19 Jun 2024) and Maximo subsystem rationalisation (25 Nov 2024) have each delayed the award.
Champions Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) and Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) are active but neither controls budget; without CFO sponsorship they cannot close.
Forecast is Best Case but lacks an engaged EB, a confirmed 2026 budget and locked commercials, so the category is indefensible; Pipeline is the honest read.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is articulated qualitatively but never quantified. Globe is looking to displace SAP Ariba across Source-to-Pay (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest per Products__c), driven by approaching Ariba renewals (Joseph Ortega, per 6 Jun 2026 Next Step) and a CEO-level appetite for AI-driven transformation (31 Jul 2024 Next Step: the CEO is eager for AI but thinks his team are not mature enough and needs our help). No ROI, savings, cycle-time or headcount figure has been stated in any field or activity, and the 6 Jun 2026 value cut from 650K to 350K makes a hard value case even more important. Quantifying the switching value versus the Ariba renewal cost is the move that converts this to confirmed.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped but not engaged: Rizza Eala (CFO, Decision Maker contact role, rmeala@globe.com.ph) is the Carl met on 31 Jul 2024 alongside the then CEO and Finance Controller Walter Ricarte, but there is no CFO-level engagement in the ~23 months since, and repeated management turnover (new CEO/CFO/CIO settled 22 Aug 2024) reset the relationship. Budget authority now sits under group-wide capex prioritisation (19 Jun 2024: Jerry advised they are reviewing multiple project costings to prioritise capex). The 6 Jun 2026 plan (get back in front of Jerry and Anton) targets procurement and the AI office, not finance. Re-securing a CFO conversation to confirm a 2026 budget line is the gating move, and it belongs to the account owner (Carl, VP APAC) who already holds the relationship.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are inferred from a long presales history rather than a documented RFP, because the formal RFP was never issued. The buyer ran extensive module deep-dives (7 May 2024: series of presentations to deep-dive all modules; 13 May 2024: demos split into 7-8 functional areas) and three days of functional/technical/commercial workshops at GCash (7 Aug 2024), and IT ran an Internal Tech Implication Assessment on Ariba-linked subsystems and Maximo (25 Oct 2024). AI capability is an explicit stated criterion: the buyer filed formal AI clarifications within the tender (21 Jul 2025 'add info on AI requested', 1 Aug 2025 'AI clarification responded') and the Chief AI Officer runs an internal AI Council (23 Sep 2024 AI workshop request). What is missing is a confirmed, written requirements set, since the criteria live in Zycus's own requirements doc that the revised RFP was meant to be based on (17 Mar).

decision_process.narrative: The buying process is procurement-led but stalled on a never-issued RFP and repeated re-org resets. Jerry Cabiles (VP Procurement) drives the process (31 Jul 2024: Jerry driving the process, wants a workshop), Rogeliza Rana runs sourcing under him, and Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) now carries internal presentations to management (6 Jun 2026). The award has been gated four separate ways over two years: leadership turnover (22 Aug 2024), group capex prioritisation (19 Jun 2024), Maximo/subsystem rationalisation (25 Nov 2024), and an IT tech-implication assessment (25 Oct 2024). The current state is reboot mode (5 Jan handover to Luk; May 2026 restart). The next real process gate is issuance of the revised RFP that Zycus's requirements are meant to seed (17 Mar), which has not landed.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics for Globe itself are largely unknown because the deal has not reached commercials since the reboot. The clearest historical signal is the intent to combine GCash and Globe into a single contract (10 Jun 2024) and legal engagement on the GCash side (CLM demo for the legal team, 14 Jun 2024), with named legal contacts on the account (Ria Caganda-Vistan, AVP Head of Business Enabling & M&A Legal Services; Luwell Manlapig, Head of Contract Management; Vin Dancel, Lawyer). No MSA/SOW status, procurement channel, or InfoSec track is yet defined for the Globe award. Issuing the RFP and reaching vendor selection is what would create paper-process visibility.
sources: OpportunityContactRole Ria Caganda-Vistan / Luwell Manlapig / Vin Dancel

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is the approaching SAP Ariba renewal decision and a mandate to modernise procurement with AI, articulated qualitatively. Joseph Ortega is presenting Zycus internally because Ariba renewals are approaching (6 Jun 2026), and the CEO wants AI-driven transformation but views his team as not yet mature enough (31 Jul 2024). Inferred pain includes integration complexity across Ariba-linked subsystems and Maximo (IT assessment 25 Oct 2024) and capex pressure across the group (19 Jun 2024). The pain owner is procurement leadership (Jerry Cabiles VP, Rogeliza Rana, Joseph Ortega), with the AI mandate owned at board level via the relevant stakeholder (Chief AI Officer). No dollar or efficiency figure has been attached to the pain.

champion.narrative: Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) is the confirmed champion: she confidentially indicated Zycus was leading the evaluation at the Summit (16 Sep 2024), brokered a warm intro to the new CFO (13 Feb), and is named in the 6 Jun 2026 F2F plan to consolidate Zycus mindshare. Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) has developed into an active second advocate, sending inbound inquiries (13 Apr, 29 May, 2 Jun 2026 on the Supply Chain Risk Management module) and offering to present Zycus internally to management as Ariba renewals near. What is developed: two engaged procurement advocates driving internal presentations; what is still missing: neither controls budget and their access to CFO Rizza Eala has not been exercised recently, so champion strength has not yet converted into executive sponsorship.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the single strongest current threat: the contract was extended 3 months (22 Aug 2024) and renewals are approaching (6 Jun 2026), so the real risk is a low-friction do-nothing renewal rather than a switch to a new platform, and Carl explicitly pulse-checked whether Globe will stay with SAP/Ariba on 18 Mar 2026. Coupa is a named alternative that ran an evaluation workshop before Zycus (31 Jul 2024) but shows no recent buyer lean and has faded. iValua appears only in the Competitors__c field with no call or Next Step corroboration and is dormant. Zycus's wedge is the agentic AI/Merlin narrative (a Chief AI Officer audience and formal AI clarifications), the GCash subsidiary reference win, and prior champion advocacy indicating Zycus was leading in 2024; the way to win is to make the switching-cost case against the Ariba renewal and land the AI differentiation the incumbent cannot match before the renewal locks.

deal_movement.summary: Reads the pulse from Next_Step_History__c, field history and the last real buyer touch: procurement inbound is live (Joseph Ortega on the SC Risk module, 2 Jun 2026) but the deal is being re-commercialised downward, not advanced. Value halved to 350K and ownership churned (Laurice Quintero to Carl Kimball, 28 Apr 2026) during a two-year reboot.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba (incumbent) is the single strongest current threat: contract extended 3 months and renewals approaching per 6 Jun 2026, so the dominant risk is a do-nothing renewal, not a rival platform, which Carl himself flagged on 18 Mar 2026. Coupa ran a workshop before Zycus (31 Jul 2024) but shows no recent buyer lean and has faded. iValua is named only in the Competitors__c field with no corroborating evidence and is dormant. Zycus was indicated to be leading in 2024 (Rogeliza, 16 Sep 2024) with the AI/Merlin story and the GCash reference as the wedge.

best_practice_check.summary: Two-year reboot with an entrenched incumbent whose renewal window is the real clock. Highest-impact levers now: re-open the CFO to confirm 2026 budget, force the RFP to actually issue, and beat the Ariba renewal decision with a dated AI/switching-cost case.
flags: Re-open CFO Rizza Eala to confirm a 2026 budget line: the economic buyer has been dark since Jul 2024 while the deal was re-commercialised down to 350K.
Convert the never-issued RFP into a dated commitment: it has been 'imminent' since Sep 2024, and Zycus's own requirements are meant to seed it.
Attach a quantified switching-cost and AI value case to beat the do-nothing Ariba renewal before the renewal window closes.

Merlin/agentic AI is well positioned but the product scope contains no AI SKUs, so the narrative must be converted into a scoped, priced AI proposal.

No Avoma calls exist to over/under-rate it against, but activity evidence corroborates genuine AI appetite.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Two-year reboot at Globe: two active procurement champions (Rogeliza Rana, Joseph Ortega) keep Zycus alive as SAP Ariba's renewal nears, but the CFO has been dark since Jul 2024, the RFP has never issued, and Carl just halved the deal to 350K, so the real fight is beating a do-nothing renewal, not a rival.

win_position.text: Why this number: Shortlisted with two strong procurement champions and an AI wedge would earn mid-60s, but we hold it lower because the economic buyer (CFO Rizza Eala) has been unengaged since 31 Jul 2024 and no 2026 budget is confirmed.

Rogeliza Rana confidentially told Carl at the CPO Summit that Zycus was leading the evaluation (16 Sep 2024), and Joseph Ortega is now presenting Zycus internally as Ariba renewals approach (6 Jun 2026).
The dominant loss path is a low-friction SAP Ariba do-nothing renewal, which Carl himself pulse-checked on 18 Mar 2026 ('will Globe stay with SAP/Ariba for now, and have renewed?').
Carl cut the deal from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026 under group capex prioritisation, a defensive commercial signal that drags the win read down.

deal_momentum.text: The formal RFP has been 'imminent' since Sep 2024 and still has not issued; the latest mention is 17 Mar (Rog expects a revised RFP soon based on Zycus's requirements).
The last rep touches are Horizon Singapore event invites logged as 'Call Attempted' (15 Jun 2026), and buyer inbound is a single module question (Joseph Ortega, 2 Jun 2026), not stage progression.
The close date has slipped five times (Aug 2025 through Dec 2026), the clearest signal that momentum is stalled rather than building.

customer_commitment.text: Joseph Ortega proactively re-engaged on the Supply Chain Risk Management module (13 Apr, 29 May, 2 Jun 2026) and offered to present Zycus internally to Jerry and Roge.
Commitment is procurement-only: CFO Rizza Eala and the finance line (Walter Ricarte) have not engaged since Jul 2024, so there is no executive sign-off behind the champions.
The buyer has not committed to a dated decision or RFP issuance; the planned F2F with Anton, Jerry, Rogelisa and Joseph is still unconfirmed (6 Jun 2026).

deal_risk.text: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent with renewals approaching (contract extended 3 months, 22 Aug 2024); a do-nothing renewal is the most likely way this is lost.
Economic buyer unengaged for ~23 months and 2026 budget unconfirmed under capex prioritisation, compounded by the 650K to 350K value cut.
Integration exposure across Ariba-linked subsystems and Maximo (IT assessment 25 Oct 2024) remains unproven and could surface technical risk at evaluation.

deal_momentum.evidence: RFP never issued after two years; close date slipped five times; last touches are event invites.

customer_commitment.evidence: Active procurement champions but no executive sign-off and no dated decision commitment.

deal_risk.evidence: Do-nothing Ariba renewal, dark CFO, halved value and unproven integration.

Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.

reasons.detail: GLOBE TELECOM is a $350k forecasted Best-Case opportunity at Shortlisted, but shows zero logged activity, movements, or calls in the 14-day window and the last touch was 18 days ago. The next step still sits on a 6 Jun plan to lock a Manila F2F with Jerry, Rogeliza, Joseph and Anton that has not advanced, while both champions (Rogeliza, Joseph) lack budget authority and the CFO (Rizza Eala) has had no engagement in ~23 months. If the silence holds, the approaching SAP Ariba renewal becomes a low-friction do-nothing and we lose the wedge before the 2026 evaluation.

reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Carl why the Globe Telecom Manila F2F (Jerry/Rogeliza/Joseph/Anton) has stalled 18 days and whether the CFO Rizza Eala re-open via Rogeliza is moving before the Ariba renewal locks.
reasons.summary: $350k Best-Case Globe Telecom deal gone quiet 18 days, next step unmoved
reasons.evidence: days_since_last_activity=18 (>14 gone quiet); recent_movements_14d, recent_activities_14d and recent_calls_14d all empty; next_step_updated_in_window=false; next step still on the unexecuted 6 Jun F2F plan.

items.action: Carl re-opens CFO Rizza Eala via Rogeliza to confirm a 2026 budget line.
items.trigger: Economic buyer dark since Jul 2024 while value was cut to 350K under capex prioritisation.
items.expected_effect: Confirms budget authority and makes the Best Case forecast defensible; without it the deal cannot close.

items.action: Ask Jerry Cabiles to commit a firm RFP issuance date seeded from Zycus's requirements.
items.trigger: Rog expects revised RFP soon, Zycus requirements are the basis, but it has slipped since Sep 2024.
items.expected_effect: Turns a two-year 'imminent' RFP into a dated process gate and restarts the evaluation clock.

items.action: Deliver a quantified switching-cost and Merlin AI case to beat the Ariba do-nothing renewal.
items.trigger: Carl pulse-checked whether Globe will stay with SAP/Ariba and renew (18 Mar 2026).
items.expected_effect: Attaches ROI to the pain and gives champions ammunition against a low-friction incumbent renewal.

items.action: Confirm the F2F with Anton, Jerry, Rogelisa and Joseph and secure Horizon Singapore attendance.
items.trigger: Planning a F2F with Anton, Jerry, Rogelisa and Joseph asap; Horizon invites sent 15 Jun 2026.
items.expected_effect: Consolidates mindshare across procurement and the AI office and multi-threads to the Chief AI Officer.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — Carl Kimball halved the amount from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026 under group-wide capex prioritisation (19 Jun 2024), a defensive signal pointing to a cost-cut or (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
customer requested next meeting (from call evidence).
Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 50 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Carl Kimball halved the amount from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026 under group-wide capex prioritisation (19 Jun….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 56 (moving forward): customer requested next meeting (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 26 is anchored on the stage win (50), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 56, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.header: GLOBE TELECOM, INC — Shortlisted · $350K · closes 31 Dec
cro_panel.intro: Two-year reboot at Globe: two active procurement champions (Rogeliza Rana, Joseph Ortega) keep Zycus alive as SAP Ariba's renewal nears, but the CFO has been dark since Jul 2024, the RFP has never issued, and Carl just halved the deal to 350K, so the real fight is beating a do-nothing renewal, not a rival.

blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: Why this number: Shortlisted with two strong procurement champions and an AI wedge would earn mid-60s, but we hold it lower because the economic buyer (CFO Rizza Eala) has been unengaged since 31 Jul 2024 and no 2026 budget is confirmed.

Rogeliza Rana confidentially told Carl at the CPO Summit that Zycus was leading the evaluation (16 Sep 2024), and Joseph Ortega is now presenting Zycus internally as Ariba renewals approach (6 Jun 2026).
The dominant loss path is a low-friction SAP Ariba do-nothing renewal, which Carl himself pulse-checked on 18 Mar 2026 ('will Globe stay with SAP/Ariba for now, and have renewed?').
Carl cut the deal from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026 under group capex prioritisation, a defensive commercial signal that drags the win read down.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 50.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Lukewarm — some motion, but not strong.

bullets.text: The formal RFP has been 'imminent' since Sep 2024 and still has not issued; the latest mention is 17 Mar (Rog expects a revised RFP soon based on Zycus's requirements).
The last rep touches are Horizon Singapore event invites logged as 'Call Attempted' (15 Jun 2026), and buyer inbound is a single module question (Joseph Ortega, 2 Jun 2026), not stage progression.
The close date has slipped five times (Aug 2025 through Dec 2026), the clearest signal that momentum is stalled rather than building.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.

bullets.text: SAP Ariba is the entrenched incumbent with renewals approaching (contract extended 3 months, 22 Aug 2024); a do-nothing renewal is the most likely way this is lost.
Economic buyer unengaged for ~23 months and 2026 budget unconfirmed under capex prioritisation, compounded by the 650K to 350K value cut.
Integration exposure across Ariba-linked subsystems and Maximo (IT assessment 25 Oct 2024) remains unproven and could surface technical risk at evaluation.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: SAP Ariba (incumbent) is the single strongest current threat: contract extended 3 months and renewals approaching per 6 Jun 2026, so the dominant risk is a do-nothing renewal, not a rival platform, which Carl himself flagged on 18 Mar 2026.

blocks.title: Customer commitment
blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.

bullets.text: Joseph Ortega proactively re-engaged on the Supply Chain Risk Management module (13 Apr, 29 May, 2 Jun 2026) and offered to present Zycus internally to Jerry and Roge.
Commitment is procurement-only: CFO Rizza Eala and the finance line (Walter Ricarte) have not engaged since Jul 2024, so there is no executive sign-off behind the champions.
The buyer has not committed to a dated decision or RFP issuance; the planned F2F with Anton, Jerry, Rogelisa and Joseph is still unconfirmed (6 Jun 2026).

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Carl re-opens CFO Rizza Eala via Rogeliza to confirm a 2026 budget line. (by 13 Jul)
Ask Jerry Cabiles to commit a firm RFP issuance date seeded from Zycus's requirements. (by 20 Jul)
Deliver a quantified switching-cost and Merlin AI case to beat the Ariba do-nothing renewal. (by 3 Aug)

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: Next_Step_History full trail (Apr 2023 to Jun 2026); activities last 120d; field history trailing 365d
gaps: No Avoma calls linked or discoverable for this opportunity; all synthesis is from Salesforce Next Step, activities, field history and contact roles.
No quantified business case / ROI stated anywhere.
Economic buyer (CFO Rizza Eala) unengaged since Jul 2024; 2026 budget unconfirmed.
